findings.title: Practice-profile setup deliberately captures firm-specific playbooks, escalation rules, risk posture, and exemplar-derived house style

evidence.description: The interview learns the program's DPA playbook, PIA style, regulatory footprint, and writes user-approved answers into a persistent profile read by every command.
The generated profile structurally records standard/fallback/never positions, policy commitments, PIA sign-off, DSAR systems, escalation routes, and seed-document locations.
Litigation setup persists risk calibration, privilege conventions, house style, matter ledger conventions, and user-approved seed-derived patterns.

source_to_sink: Attorney answers and uploaded seed documents
Every downstream skill reads the profile
findings.proves: The repository is expressly designed to encode substantial institution-specific operating knowledge and legal-judgment scaffolds in persistent configuration.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not establish provider-side retention, generalized model training, cross-customer pooling, ownership transfer, or covert access to the local profile.
findings.alternative_explanation: This is ordinary customer-controlled personalization intended to improve requested legal work.
findings.test_to_confirm: Trace the deployed runtime's file reads, model request payloads, retention controls, and tenant boundaries while invoking a profile-dependent skill.

findings.title: Deviation history is converted into proposed reusable playbook policy through a gated feedback loop

evidence.description: The agent watches deviations after deal debriefs and triggers on repeated clause-level departures.
It reads the current playbook and deviation log, groups direction and basis, and drafts specific revisions supported by deal data.
Accept/edit/reject/defer workflow requires exact-diff display and explicit attorney confirmation before modifying the playbook.

source_to_sink: Deal outcomes and stated deviation bases
Pattern grouping by clause, direction, and basis
findings.proves: The code defines a concrete learning loop from attorney deal behavior to reusable operating policy and thus captures a meaningful slice of institutional judgment.
findings.does_not_prove: The loop is prompt-defined rather than runtime-observed and supplies no evidence that proposals or logs leave the customer's local environment or train a shared model.
findings.alternative_explanation: A locally governed continuous-improvement loop can preserve firm knowledge without provider secondary use.
findings.test_to_confirm: Run a synthetic debrief cohort with file and network tracing; verify local writes, notification payloads, approval enforcement, and provider retention.

findings.title: Matter files persist client facts, decisions, outputs, and archives with explicit default cross-matter isolation

evidence.description: Matter commands create, switch, archive, and retain matter files; cross-matter reads require an enabled flag.
Storage includes matter facts, dated histories, notes, outputs, archived matters, and a practice-level active-matter pointer.
Cross-matter context is off by default and, even when on, requires an explicit user request; retention policy remains out of scope.

source_to_sink: Client and matter intake and skill outputs
findings.proves: The design creates a durable local corpus of matter-specific facts, choices, work product, and history while specifying a confidentiality boundary.
findings.does_not_prove: Prompt instructions alone do not prove runtime enforcement, deletion compliance, privilege preservation, encryption, or that local content is never transmitted for inference.
findings.alternative_explanation: Persistent matter workspaces are necessary customer-side records, and the default isolation rules weigh against indiscriminate pooling.
findings.test_to_confirm: Use canary data in two matters and trace reads and model payloads under cross-matter off and on states, archive operations, and close and reopen flows.

findings.title: Configured MCP connectors create potential remote source and sink boundaries for legal and workplace data

evidence.description: Slack and Google Drive HTTP MCP endpoints are configured for workspace messages and documents.
Litigation adds Everlaw, TopCounsel, CourtListener, Aurora, and Trellis remote MCP endpoints, including e-discovery and cross-matter investigation services.
The builder hub configures a third-party Lawve AI skills registry endpoint.

source_to_sink: Authorized workspace, document, e-discovery, or research data
Unknown service-side logging, retention, and secondary use
findings.proves: The repository declares remote connector boundaries capable of reading sensitive enterprise/legal sources when authorized and invoked.
findings.does_not_prove: Configuration is not connection or invocation; it does not reveal credentials, payload contents, provider retention, Anthropic access, covert exfiltration, or training.
findings.alternative_explanation: Connectors are user-authorized tools necessary to fetch requested records and research; remote processing may be contractually and technically isolated.
findings.test_to_confirm: Capture deployed MCP authorization scopes, per-call payloads, operator identity, logs, retention terms, and tenant-isolation controls.

findings.title: Wildcard messaging tools permit agent notifications but do not establish transmission of underlying legal artifacts

evidence.description: The monitor has Read, Write, wildcard notify, and wildcard Slack-send tools.
It writes proposal and run-log files and sends a summary notification pointing to the local proposal path.
A weekly registry agent has Read, Write, WebFetch, and wildcard Slack-send access for update digests.

findings.proves: Some agents combine local read authority with broad notification tool patterns, creating an outbound channel whose payload discipline matters.
findings.does_not_prove: The defined monitor notification contains counts and a local path, not the underlying deal data; no evidence shows secrets are sent or retained by Anthropic.
findings.alternative_explanation: Wildcard names accommodate connector implementations, while the prompt limits notifications to operational digests.
findings.test_to_confirm: Instrument every tool call and verify payload allowlists, destination resolution, connector scopes, and whether prompt constraints are enforced outside model discretion.

findings.title: Community-skill update workflow is a supply-chain surface with substantial documented human and structural gates

evidence.description: Updater treats skills as privileged code, pins commit SHAs, shows full diffs, and flags hooks, MCP, permissions, network, writes, and purpose changes.
New versions are scanned read-only; security regressions fail closed and REFUSE-tier updates have no override.
Restrictive mode refuses unapproved registries, publishers, and connectors and evaluates allowlist metadata before attacker-controlled skill content.

source_to_sink: External registry content at immutable commit
Administrator allowlist and explicit human approval
findings.proves: The code recognizes and attempts to constrain a serious update/registry supply-chain boundary.
findings.does_not_prove: Prompt-described gates are not proof of atomic enforcement, correct parser behavior, signature verification, sandboxing, or uncompromised publishers.
findings.alternative_explanation: These controls materially support H0 by making third-party code adoption visible and human-gated.
findings.test_to_confirm: Adversarially test mutable refs, compromised trusted publisher, injected metadata, MCP/tool expansion, partial writes, rollback, and approval bypass against the deployed installer.

findings.title: User-scoped installation broadens where skills can operate, while documentation claims explicit-pointing/current-directory limits

evidence.description: Quickstart recommends user scope because project scope blocks access to files in Downloads, Documents, Dropbox, and other project-external locations.
Persistent practice profile is described as user-owned and all outputs as attorney-reviewed drafts.

source_to_sink: Model/tool reads for the requested workflow
Output returned to user or local configured destination
findings.proves: The intended deployment can process files across a user's broader filesystem rather than one project boundary.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not show ambient crawling, unauthorized reads, remote retention, or the technical truth of the stated explicit-pointing restriction.
findings.alternative_explanation: Legal users commonly keep requested matter files outside a source-code project; user scope is a usability choice.
findings.test_to_confirm: Inspect actual Claude Code/Cowork filesystem sandbox and audit read attempts with unreferenced canary files under user-scoped and project-scoped installs.

findings.title: Managed-agent examples demonstrate least-privilege decomposition and injection-aware output handling

evidence.description: Docket reader treats filings as untrusted data, follows no links, has no Write/non-docket MCP access, and returns schema-limited fields.
Tools are default-disabled, two named MCPs are enabled, and strict output schemas constrain returned records and URL hosts.
Only writer sanitizes formula/HTML/Markdown hazards, emits inert upstream URLs, and has no MCP servers or callable agents.

source_to_sink: Read-only bounded specialist and schema validation
Sanitizing writer with no external connector
findings.proves: The repository contains concrete architectural controls against prompt injection, overbroad writes, and unsafe rendering.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not establish that all workflows use these patterns or that the proprietary orchestrator enforces the declared tool restrictions exactly.
findings.alternative_explanation: These are affirmative security controls that weigh against a generalized hostile-design characterization.
findings.test_to_confirm: Run adversarial filings/tickets through the deployed agents and verify tool-denial logs, schema rejection, URL handling, and rendered output safety.

findings.title: Repository evidence does not complete a covert-training or cross-customer secondary-use chain

evidence.description: The code teaches users to investigate training, input confidentiality, retention flow-down, and stacked-provider responsibility rather than asserting those provider facts.
Setup states it uses typed or approved material and does not pull personal history or ambient home configuration.
Monitor explicitly forbids unconfirmed playbook changes and limits pattern promotion.

source_to_sink: Inference-time reads are visible in prompt design
Generalized training or cross-customer product use evidence: absent
findings.proves: The assigned source establishes rich inference-time personalization and local persistence but leaves the retention and secondary-use steps unobserved.
findings.does_not_prove: Absence in this repository is not proof of zero telemetry, zero retention, contract compliance, or impossibility of secondary use in closed services.
findings.alternative_explanation: Observed behavior is consistent with H0 customer-specific inference; H1 remains plausible through dependency/product insight, while H2 is not established.
findings.test_to_confirm: Obtain authenticated product terms/DPA, data-retention configuration, telemetry schemas, network captures, access logs, deletion tests, and provider-side training/product governance evidence.

findings.title: pull_request_target workflow grants write permissions to a pinned third-party action

evidence.description: Workflow runs on pull_request_target and grants actions, contents, pull-request, and status write permissions.
A third-party CLA action is SHA-pinned and receives GITHUB_TOKEN.

findings.proves: The repository CI delegates write-capable token authority to a specific third-party action commit.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not show compromise, legal-client data access, Anthropic telemetry, or a path from installed legal plugins to this CI workflow.
findings.alternative_explanation: The action is immutable-SHA pinned and used for routine CLA administration.
findings.test_to_confirm: Audit the pinned action commit and transitive dependencies; reduce permissions to minimum and test untrusted PR/comment payload handling.

new_patterns.name: Judgment-loop promotion
new_patterns.logic: Find workflows that aggregate deviations, corrections, accept/reject outcomes, or rationales and propose persistent policy changes.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Ordinary logs may lack any promotion into reusable policy; require both aggregation and a persistent update sink.

new_patterns.name: Local corpus plus outbound notifier
new_patterns.logic: Identify an agent with Read over sensitive local artifacts and a notify/send tool, then inspect the exact allowed payload.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Notification may contain only benign counts or local pointers; do not infer artifact transmission without payload evidence.

new_patterns.name: Cross-matter gate semantics
new_patterns.logic: Compare default flags, explicit-request conditions, practice-level promotion, archive readability, and actual tool/runtime enforcement.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Prompt statements may be duplicated templates and not enforced by a non-model access-control layer.

new_patterns.name: Connector capability versus observed connection
new_patterns.logic: Separate declared MCP endpoint, successful authorization/probe, actual invocation, payload, operator retention, and secondary use.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: A configured endpoint alone is neither connected nor a data flow.

new_patterns.name: External-code trust transition
new_patterns.logic: Trace registry metadata through allowlist, immutable revision, diff, scan, approval, installation, backup, and rollback.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Documentation may describe gates not implemented in deterministic code.

negative_evidence.description: Every playbook mutation requires per-change attorney confirmation; rejected and one-off deviations are not automatically promoted.
negative_evidence.effect: Weighs strongly against autonomous extraction-and-rewrite interpretations and supports H0, while leaving inference payloads untested.

negative_evidence.description: Cross-matter reads are off by default and require explicit user request even when enabled.
negative_evidence.effect: Contradicts a claim that the source instructs indiscriminate matter pooling.

negative_evidence.description: Profile population is limited to typed answers, seed documents, and separately approved conversational facts; ambient personal history is expressly excluded.
negative_evidence.effect: Contradicts an asserted design intent to silently ingest unrelated user context.

negative_evidence.description: Untrusted filings are data, tool access is default-disabled, Write is absent, output is schema-restricted, and URL hosts are constrained.
negative_evidence.effect: Shows concrete least-privilege and injection controls.

negative_evidence.description: Immutable commits, full diffs, read-only rescans, mandatory human approval, fail-closed regression behavior, and no REFUSE override are specified.
negative_evidence.effect: Substantially reduces but does not eliminate update supply-chain risk.

negative_evidence.description: The skill requires checking actual contract terms for training, retention, confidentiality, and provider flow-down rather than presuming them.
negative_evidence.effect: Demonstrates epistemic separation between product capability and contractual secondary-use evidence.

unknowns.question: Are practice profiles, matter files, deviation logs, prompts, outputs, or tool traces retained by Anthropic or any connector beyond the requested service?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The repository describes prompt behavior and local paths, not proprietary runtime telemetry, service storage, or operator logs.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Authenticated terms/DPA, retention configuration, network traces, telemetry schemas, access logs, deletion attestations, and connector contracts.

unknowns.question: Are customer-specific legal signals used for generalized model improvement or cross-customer product learning?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: No assigned file identifies a provider-side training dataset, ingestion job, feedback pipeline, pooling key, or secondary-use policy.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Training-data governance records, dataset lineage, opt-in records, product analytics schemas, model cards/change logs, and controlled canary experiments.

unknowns.question: Do prompt-declared matter isolation and tool restrictions hold in the deployed proprietary runtime?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The source provides instructions/manifests but no enforcement logs or runtime implementation.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Adversarial runtime tests with canary matters, tool-denial logs, sandbox policy, and model request payload captures.

unknowns.question: What data do remote MCP operators log, retain, or reuse, and under whose contract?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: MCP configurations list URLs and capabilities only.
unknowns.needed_evidence: OAuth scopes, per-call captures, operator DPAs/terms, subprocessors, retention/deletion controls, audit reports, and tenant-isolation evidence.

unknowns.question: Does the updater/installer implement the documented gates atomically and resist malicious metadata and trusted-publisher compromise?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: Assigned materials are behavioral instructions and reference policy, not the full deployed executor or test evidence.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Implementation audit plus adversarial test receipts for parser confusion, prompt injection, approval bypass, partial writes, signature/provenance, and rollback.
